skill.name: simple-brainstorm
skill.description: A lightweight brainstorming process for creative, product, and architecture decisions before implementation begins.
skill.summary: Guide fast, focused brainstorming before design or implementation work starts.
skill.author: roin-orca

security_audit.summary: Manual review found no confirmed security issue. The external command alert is a fenced DOT diagram, and the weak cryptography alerts are prose false positives with no cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Diagram Fenced Block
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but SKILL.md lines 17-30 are a fenced DOT diagram. No command is executed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside a markdown code fence labeled dot and only defines graph nodes and edges. No shell, Ruby, script, or executable file is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static weak cryptography alerts matched ordinary planning prose in SKILL.md. No hashing, encryption, random generation, or security-sensitive algorithm appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only instructional text about brainstorming and design decisions. The empty auxiliary files contain no code, so there is no cryptographic implementation to evaluate.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose time when creative or architecture choices are unclear. This skill creates a short decision process that explores options, compares trade-offs, and captures a direction.
actual_capabilities: Guides a Discover, Propose, Converge, and Capture brainstorming flow.
Asks up to three focused clarification questions when context is missing.
Presents two practical approaches with trade-offs and a recommendation.
Limits proposal revision to two rounds before asking for direct direction.
Captures the chosen direction in chat or as an inline note when implementation starts.
limitations: It does not perform external research or validate facts from the web.
It does not implement code before the user approves a direction.
It is best for small and medium decisions, not formal enterprise architecture review.
It depends on the user to provide project goals, constraints, and success criteria.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Feature
use_cases.description: Explore the goal, constraints, and two implementation paths before coding begins.

use_cases.title: Choose a Component Design
use_cases.description: Compare two designs for a component and select the simpler path that fits current requirements.

use_cases.title: Validate a Behavior Change
use_cases.description: Clarify expected behavior, trade-offs, and approval before changing an existing workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Brainstorm
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me brainstorm a direction for this task: [describe the task]. Ask only the key questions first.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a focused decision session

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Options
prompt_templates.prompt: I am choosing between [option A] and [option B] for [goal]. Compare trade-offs and recommend one.

prompt_templates.title: Shape a Feature Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Before implementation, help me design [feature]. Consider user goal, project constraints, risks, and the simplest viable approach.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a new feature

prompt_templates.title: Resolve an Architecture Decision
prompt_templates.prompt: Use a concise brainstorm to evaluate architecture paths for [system area]. Include assumptions, rejected options, and a captured decision.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced technical decision with durable context

output_examples.input: I need to decide how to add saved filters to a dashboard.
output_examples.output: The skill would ask about user roles, storage scope, and sharing needs. It would then compare a local-only approach against a persisted server-side approach and recommend the simplest fit.

output_examples.input: Should our new settings panel be modal or full page?
output_examples.output: The skill would compare speed, focus, accessibility, and future complexity. It would recommend a full page if settings are expected to grow.

output_examples.input: We need to change checkout behavior but I am unsure where to start.
output_examples.output: The skill would clarify the business goal, affected users, and rollback needs. It would capture the approved direction before implementation begins.

best_practices: Provide goals, constraints, and known alternatives in the first message.
Ask for a recommendation when trade-offs are close.
Capture the approved direction before implementation begins.
anti_patterns: Using the skill after code has already been written for the decision.
Requesting many speculative future-proof options with no current requirement.
Skipping user approval before moving from discussion to implementation.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No. It helps choose a direction first. Implementation should start only after explicit user approval.

faq.question: How many options does it compare?
faq.answer: It usually compares two approaches and leads with a recommendation.

faq.question: Can it help with architecture decisions?
faq.answer: Yes, for focused architecture choices. Large reviews may need a more formal process.

faq.question: Does it require a design document?
faq.answer: No. It captures the chosen direction in chat or an inline note unless the user requests more.

faq.question: Is it useful for non-code work?
faq.answer: Yes. The process can support product, writing, design, and workflow decisions.

faq.question: Which tools support this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
